title: Stream API keys should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Stream API key detected in source code. Stream (GetStream.io) is a real-time activity feed and chat API platform used for building scalable social feeds, messaging, and notification systems. API keys provide full access to your Stream application including: reading and modifying activity feeds, accessing chat messages and conversations, managing user data and permissions, controlling feed groups and aggregations, accessing analytics and metrics, modifying application configuration, and accessing real-time events. Exposing these keys allows unauthorized users to: read private messages and activity data, inject false activities and spam content, delete or modify feeds and conversations, access sensitive user data and analytics, exhaust API rate limits and quotas, modify application settings, impersonate users in feeds and chat, and potentially cause service disruption. Store API keys securely using environment variables (STREAM_API_KEY, STREAM_SECRET) or secret management systems (HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault, GCP Secret Manager). IMMEDIATELY revoke exposed keys from Stream Dashboard (dashboard.getstream.io → App Settings → API Keys) and regenerate new credentials. Use separate keys for development and production environments, implement proper API key rotation policies, enable rate limiting, monitor API usage for anomalies, use Feed tokens with limited permissions for client-side access, and never commit keys to version control or expose in client-side code.

Stream API key detected in variable assignment. Stream (GetStream.io) provides real-time activity feeds and chat APIs. API keys grant full access to your application's feeds, messages, user data, and configuration. These keys should never be hardcoded in source code. Use environment variables or secret management systems instead. Immediately revoke this key from Stream Dashboard and regenerate new credentials.
